tests.fr_description: Foreign process on port not reported as success with dead child PID

scenarios.given: no PID file exists, a foreign process holds the configured port
scenarios.when: startServer() spawns child that dies (EADDRINUSE) while waitForServer gets 200 from foreign process
scenarios.then: startServer returns { success: false, foreignPort: true } and no PID file written
scenarios.test_name: [P0] AC-1: Given foreign process on port, When child dies with EADDRINUSE and waitForServer gets 200 from foreign process, Then startServer returns failure with foreignPort flag and no PID file written

scenarios.given: clean spawn scenario with no port conflicts
scenarios.then: PID file write is deferred until after child aliveness confirmation via process.kill(pid, 0)
scenarios.test_name: [P0] AC-1/AC-2: Given successful spawn, When startServer completes, Then PID file write is deferred until after child aliveness confirmation

tests.fr_description: Stale PID file + port squatter results in failure, not spurious success

scenarios.given: stale PID file exists (PID not alive), foreign process holds port
scenarios.when: startServer() cleans stale PID, spawns child that dies, waitForServer gets 200 from foreign process
scenarios.then: returns failure, stale PID file not replaced with dead child PID
scenarios.test_name: [P0] AC-2: Given stale PID file and port squatter, When startServer spawns child that dies but port responds, Then failure is reported and PID file not written with dead child PID

tests.fr_description: Normal clean spawn still works correctly (regression guard)

scenarios.given: no PID file, no foreign process, port is free
scenarios.when: startServer() spawns child that stays alive, waitForServer gets 200
scenarios.then: returns { success: true, pid: child.pid } and PID file contains child.pid
scenarios.test_name: [P0] AC-3: Given clean environment with no port conflict, When startServer spawns child that stays alive and port responds, Then success with correct PID file written

scenarios.given: no PID file, port is free
scenarios.when: startServer() spawns child that stays alive but waitForServer times out
scenarios.then: returns { success: false } with timeout error, no PID file written
scenarios.test_name: [P1] AC-3: Given child stays alive but port never responds, When waitForServer times out, Then failure returned and no PID file written

tests.fr_description: Concurrent startServer calls: one wins, other detects, no dead PID

scenarios.given: lock file held by live process (simulating concurrent startup)
scenarios.when: second startServer() call detects lock held
scenarios.then: second call waits for server, does not write its own PID file
scenarios.test_name: [P1] AC-4: Given lock held by another live process, When second startServer call runs, Then it waits for server and returns lock-based result without writing PID

scenarios.given: lock file held by dead process, foreign process on port
scenarios.when: startServer() cleans stale lock, spawns child that dies, foreign process responds
scenarios.then: returns failure, does not write dead child PID
scenarios.test_name: [P1] AC-4: Given stale lock from dead process and foreign port squatter, When startServer acquires lock and child dies, Then no dead PID file written

tests.fr_description: After reload 2xx, verify /modules shows correct package loaded

scenarios.given: embedding server running, /reload returns 200, /modules returns valid package metadata (chunks > 0)
scenarios.when: F0009 completes generation and postReload verifies
scenarios.test_name: [P0] AC-5: Given reload returns 2xx and /modules shows valid package with chunks > 0, When postReload verifies, Then serverReloaded is true

scenarios.given: embedding server running, /reload returns 200, /modules returns empty or zero-chunk
scenarios.when: F0009 completes generation and postReload verifies
scenarios.then: reload_failed is true, warning emitted to stderr
scenarios.test_name: [P0] AC-5: Given reload returns 2xx but /modules shows empty or zero-chunk package, When postReload verifies, Then reload_failed is true and warning emitted
